reason: Check implementation against confirmed M3 requirements and acceptance examples.
Verify M3 preserves M1 plugin and hook behavior.
Verify provider bridge boundary remains intact and tools are not executed by provider code.
Review safety, permission, hook, and result-handling choices against reference project patterns.
Review loop behavior, tool result pairing, concurrency, and failure return semantics.
Entry point for backend/core quality checks.
Check structured errors, denials, timeouts, and hook failures follow project guidance.
Check maintainability and test coverage of backend/core changes.
Check contract changes across runtime, plugins, provider bridge, and tests.
Check new pipeline/permission abstractions avoid duplicating existing registry, hook, and event patterns.
